summary: Persistent root/SYSTEM service with remote IM command channel and unpinned 6h auto-updater
details: On install, the package registers a privileged auto-restarting system service (Windows SCM as SYSTEM via node-windows + bundled clawsubagentservice.exe, Linux systemd unit with ExecStart as root, macOS LaunchDaemon). The daemon connects outbound to a RongCloud IM channel (appKey bmdehs6pbyyks, token-refresh endpoint https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im) and dispatches inbound IM messages to a shell executor: COMMAND messages reach service/modules/script-executor.js which runs `spawn('bash', ['-c', command])` (or `cmd /c` on Windows), CONFIG_FIX runs `openclaw doctor --fix`, CHAT_MESSAGE is forwarded to a local AI gateway at 127.0.0.1:4096 whose system prompt grants shell-execution authority, and DEVICE_CONTROL.disable/delete uninstalls the service. Whoever controls the operator side of the IM channel obtains arbitrary shell execution as root/SYSTEM on every enrolled host. A separate Updater (service/updater.js) runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` every 6 hours unattended, replacing the running privileged binary with whatever is currently latest on the registry; a publish-credential compromise yields root on every installer within 6h with no re-consent. The Worker also shells out `npm install -g opencode-ai@latest` as root when opencode is missing, extending the privileged surface to a third-party unpinned package. HeartbeatManager and DashboardReporter stream MAC address, hostname, listening ports, OpenClaw AI session contents (~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl: model/provider/tokens/cost), project and task data to the IM operator every 20-30 seconds.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
